title: lasthenia glabrata subsp. coulteri
intro: Lasthenia glabrata subsp. coulteri, also known as Coulter's goldfields, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant that grows up to 2 feet tall, producing bright yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties that may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing skin irritations. Coulter's goldfields can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for various health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and boost the immune system.
